lessons.title: ClickUp assignees silently ignored on PUT
lessons.pattern: Task created with 200 OK but assignees are empty in ClickUp UI
lessons.root_cause: PUT /api/v2/task/{id} ignores the assignees field entirely. Also: wrong format [{'id': 123}] used instead of flat int list [123].
lessons.fix: Set assignees ONLY at creation time via POST /api/v2/list/{list_id}/task. Use flat integer list: [100842373], not [{'id': 100842373}].
lessons.prevention: See clickup-ops SKILL.md API Rules section. Always use create_task() canonical template.

lessons.title: ClickUp status not found error on task creation
lessons.root_cause: Status string in payload doesn't match the list's exact configured status names. E.g. sent 'to do' when list uses 'todo'.
lessons.fix: Always fetch the list first (GET /api/v2/list/{id}) and read the exact status strings from the 'statuses' array before creating tasks.
lessons.prevention: See clickup-ops SKILL.md Rule 5. Run _check_statuses script before any bulk task creation.

lessons.title: ClickUp subtask creation 404 via /subtask endpoint
lessons.root_cause: The /subtask endpoint does not exist in this ClickUp workspace configuration.
lessons.fix: Create subtasks via POST /api/v2/list/{list_id}/task with 'parent': parent_task_id in the payload.
lessons.prevention: See clickup-ops SKILL.md Rule 6 and create_subtask() canonical template.

lessons.title: ClickUp due dates off by one day in IST
lessons.pattern: Task created for Jun 9 appears as Jun 8 in ClickUp
lessons.root_cause: datetime(yyyy, mm, dd).timestamp() uses midnight UTC, which is 05:30 IST next day — ClickUp displays the UTC date.
lessons.fix: Use datetime(yyyy, mm, dd, 18, 0).timestamp() * 1000 and set due_date_time: False. 18:00 local safely falls on the correct calendar date in any timezone.
lessons.prevention: Always use the ms() helper: def ms(y,m,d): return int(datetime(y,m,d,18,0).timestamp()*1000)

lessons.title: Morning report capacity default was 40h — corrected to 48h
lessons.pattern: Weekly capacity of 40h underestimated actual work hours, causing false OVERLOADED flags and inflated alarm in reports.
lessons.root_cause: Default capacity was set to 40h in three places: hr_structure.json (21 people), generate_morning_report.py (6 fallback lines), and SKILL.md documentation.
lessons.fix: Updated all occurrences: hr_structure.json capacity_hours_per_week and available_hours_per_week 40→48; Python script fallbacks 40→48; SKILL.md docs 40h→48h.
lessons.prevention: When adjusting company-wide defaults, change all three layers: (1) hr_structure.json source of truth, (2) script fallback defaults, (3) SKILL.md documentation. Use grep to verify zero stale references remain.
